name: Lens Luxation
description: Lens luxation is displacement of the lens from its normal position due to breakdown of the suspensory ligaments (zonules). Primary lens luxation is hereditary. A luxated lens can cause acute glaucoma and retinal detachment.
treatment_overview: Surgical removal of the luxated lens (intracapsular extraction) is the treatment of choice. Medical management with miotics helps when surgery is not immediately possible. Glaucoma treatment may also be required.
prevention_tips: DNA test available (ADAMTS17 mutation) — do not breed affected dogs
